-- Step 1: Express the inner product ⟨x, a y⟩ using the continuous functional calculus.
  have h₁ : ⟪x, a y⟫_ℂ = (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y (algebraMap ℂ (C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) 1)) := by

    simp [toLinearFunctional_apply, cfc_toLinearFunctional_apply, cfcHom_isometry]
    <;>
    simp_all [ContinuousLinearMap.map_one, Complex.norm_eq_abs, Complex.abs_of_nonneg]
    <;>
    norm_num
    <;>
    simp_all [IsSelfAdjoint.map_one]
    <;>
    ring_nf
    <;>
    simp_all [inner_add_right, inner_add_left, inner_smul_right, inner_smul_left]
    <;>
    norm_num
    <;>
    linarith
  
  -- Step 2: Relate the integral of the complex measure to the continuous functional calculus.
  have h₂ : (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y (algebraMap ℂ (C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) 1)) =
    (toComplexMeasure (toResolutionOfIdentity ha) x y).integral (Function.Embedding.subtype (spectrum ℂ a)) := by
    -- Use the Riesz-Markov-Kakutani theorem to equate the linear functional with the integral of the complex measure.
    simp_all [toComplexMeasure, toResolutionOfIdentity, toBoundedBilin]
    <;>
    norm_num
    <;>
    simp_all [ComplexMeasure.integral]
    <;>
    ring_nf
    <;>
    simp_all [inner_add_right, inner_add_left, inner_smul_right, inner_smul_left]
    <;>
    norm_num
    <;>
    linarith
  
  -- Step 3: Combine the two steps to get the final result.
  calc
    ⟪x, a y⟫_ℂ = (cfc_toLinearFunctional ha x y (algebraMap ℂ (C((spectrum ℂ a), ℂ)) 1)) := by rw [h₁]
    _ = (toComplexMeasure (toResolutionOfIdentity ha) x y).integral (Function.Embedding.subtype (spectrum ℂ a)) := by rw [h₂]
